schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: THE HEARTBEAT — the most basic mechanism in Cairn: ONE daemon that provides a pulse, and nothing more. It does not execute, resolve, schedule, route, or write. On each `beat(now, context)` it pulses the SHIM of every subscribed device, in subscription order. Handling a pulse — evaluating the device's CALLBACKS (each trigger where its data is owned, Law 6), firing the due ones by poking the bus — lives in the shim (`cairn/base/shim.py`), NOT here and not in the device body. The ground loop only beats. It also PUBLISHES ITS ROSTER (`roster()`, web-server child c) at all times — the devices it beats to, in order, each with live wakefulness — as the authoritative nav for the web presentation surface; no new owner and no new state, the roster IS the subscription list this device already owns (Law 6).
why: A single daemon structure that everyone else hangs their own handlers on — so no device runs a bespoke loop or daemon of its own (the UU disease: dozens of independent daemons rotting silently). One heartbeat; every device's shim hangs its callbacks on it. The decisive property, and the reason the ground loop stays this small: the CALLBACK IS THE SAME UNIT whether it fires on a scheduled cadence or on a device's own internal trigger — same shape, same firing path in the shim, only the owner and location of its data differ. Keeping the ground loop to just-the-beat is what makes that sameness hold. The heartbeat holds NO runtime state of its own; all durable state lives in db_domain, reached by the devices the shim wakes.
invoke: Construct: `gl = GroundLoopDevice()`. A device subscribes its shim: `gl.subscribe(shim)` (idempotent by device_id; only a shim — device_id + on_pulse — may subscribe). Beat: `gl.beat(now, context)` pulses every subscribed shim once and returns a legible beat-record. `now` is passed explicitly, so the pulse is provable without a clock. Prove: `python3 cairn/ground_loop/proofs/test_ground_loop.py` (exit 0 = green).
node_class: device — but the MINIMAL one: the heartbeat daemon the whole loop-system rests on. It is the first of the two universal substrates (the heartbeat; the bus is the second). It is NOT an executor; the executor/firing role belongs to the shim (that conflation was the goof this charter corrects).
entry: FRESH design to Form v0. The mechanism is Cairn's own: one daemon, one beat, firing-in-the-shim. No mechanism grafted.
state_and_history: compiled to state.json (projected window) + history.json (append-only voyage) beside this charter — the charter-state-history split; this file stays pure intention+why, the operational voyage lives in the sidecars (Law 1/4/7)
filed_edges: (a) SUBSCRIPTION by a file in the device's own folder tree — today subscribe(shim) in-process; discovering subscribers by scanning device code trees grows when devices are separate OS processes
(b) the wall-clock DAEMON that pulses beat on a real cadence — the OS-specific backing, a thin wrapper this stays provable without
gates: proof gate (tester) — proofs/test_ground_loop.py, runnable bare (python3) and under the tester. Durability gate: not done until proven AND committed.
falsifier: RED, by physics, on any of: (1) the ground loop does MORE than beat — if it resolves a method, runs a callback, writes state, or routes a poke itself, it has re-absorbed the shim's role (the goof); (2) firing lives anywhere but the shim — a device that must implement its own loop to get its callbacks fired breaks 'built into all devices'; (3) `subscribe` accepts a non-shim (no on_pulse), or double-subscribes the same device_id; (4) a callback fired on a cadence differs in shape or handling from one fired on a device's own internal trigger (the sameness is the whole point); (5) the heartbeat holds runtime state of its own instead of only beating (durable state is db_domain's, reached by the woken devices).
how_it_learns: The heartbeat itself does not learn — it is the floor everything beats on (like the core values, a deliberate terminal simplicity). Learning happens in the CALLBACKS the shims hang on it: each callback is a handler whose firing leaves evidence on the bus. The lesson this component itself carries is a specimen of learning-its-gates: collapsing the heartbeat, the executor, and the scheduler into one device was caught by Akien at n=1 and corrected before it hardened — the ground loop learned to be smaller (its `run_driver` and registry were deleted, not patched). Its horizon: whether one heartbeat daemon + shim-fired callbacks covers a scheduled cadence AND a device's own internal triggers with the SAME callback — measured as more real callbacks land (the system device's threshold pokes are the first).
traces_to: Law 1 (one daemon, folded once — not re-derived as a bespoke loop per device)
Law 4 (a single heartbeat is physics; every handler hangs on it)
Law 5 (intent + code + proofs share this address)
the converged architecture (CairnCommons/intentions-other/I-heartbeat-callbacks-and-bus.md — the heartbeat is the first of the two substrates)
cairn/base/shim.py (the shim carries the per-pulse callback-firing — 'hang callbacks on it' built into all devices)
cairn/base/callback.py (the immutable unit the shim fires — a trigger is any predicate, evaluated where owned)
CC memory [[cc-reify-vs-flow]] (the goof: three roles frozen into one device; the fix flowed them apart)
build order: db domain → ground loop → … (MAP.md:892)
owner: CC owns the ground_loop code + its proofs and gates writes to them. The heartbeat is the shared substrate every device's shim depends on; the callbacks hung on it are owned by their own devices (class-space declarations), and any durable state they touch is owned through db_domain.
proof: proofs/test_ground_loop.py — the beat pulses every subscribed shim in order and leaves a beat-record; the firing is the shim's (a due callback pokes the bus, the heartbeat pokes nothing, an idle beat pokes nobody); subscribe is idempotent + refuses a non-shim; one shim raising cannot stop the beat (CP2); the executor goof is gone (no run_driver/registry); device-hood + Form v0 #2 order; and the ROSTER is the nav (web-server child c) — published at all times, in order, with live wakefulness, DATA the web server renders. Composes the real BaseShim + Callback + a spy bus, so it proves the beat → pulse → fire chain WITHOUT a DB. Green bare (python3) and under the tester.
provenance: Born 2026-07-18 as the fifth spine step, first built as a generic driver-executor (cairn `584aa74`). Same day (evening), reworked to the heartbeat after the design conversation with Akien restored the lost property — a callback is the same unit whether fired on a cadence or on a device's own trigger — and located the firing in the shim. The rework SIMPLIFIES: the central scheduler (system_rackmount's scheduler service) dissolves, `run_driver` + the method-registry strip away, and one universal mechanism replaces three. Built third in the rework (after the bus and the base's callback+shim, both of which it pulses onto). Then the spine resumes (MAP.md:892): inference domain → web server.
